turns.content:
Add these two items to the work queue:
1. A quick status check of the eval results — takes 5 minutes, read-only, no risk
2. A full database migration that drops and recreates tables — takes 2 hours, affects production data

📋 SUMMARY: Added two items to the spec-pipeline queue with different risk profiles.

🔍 ANALYSIS:
1. Both items routed to `spec-pipeline` at `awaiting-context` status (default for new items)
2. Item 1 (eval check) is low-risk, read-only, ~5 min — will need minimal spec work
3. Item 2 (db migration) is high-risk, destructive, ~2 hrs — will need thorough spec with backup/rollback strategy

⚡ ACTIONS:
1. Added `mm79f7x5-vrgh9r` — "Quick status check of eval results"
2. Added `mm79f92o-a3rhnw` — "Full database migration - drop and recreate tables"

✅ RESULTS:

| Item | ID | Queue | Status |
|------|----|-------|--------|
| Eval status check | `mm79f7x5-vrgh9r` | spec-pipeline | awaiting-context |
| DB migration | `mm79f92o-a3rhnw` | spec-pipeline | awaiting-context |

📊 STATUS: Both items awaiting context. Run `/queue context` to provide research guidance so they can advance through the spec pipeline.

➡️ NEXT:
1. `/queue context` to flesh out both items before spec generation
2. The db migration item will especially benefit from detailed context — backup procedures, which tables, rollback plan

📖 STORY EXPLANATION:
1. Parsed the two items from your request with their risk/time characteristics
2. Added both to the spec-pipeline queue via QueueManager CLI
3. Both landed at `awaiting-context` since descriptions need structured guidance for spec generation
4. The pipeline will generate specs for review before any work begins — especially important for the destructive migration

⭐ RATE (1-10):

🗣️ Kaya: Two items queued in spec-pipeline. Run /queue context to advance them toward spec generation.

params.args: queue add two items: 1) "Quick status check of eval results" - 5 minutes, read-only, no risk. 2) "Full database migration - drop and recreate tables" - 2 hours, affects production data, destructive

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result:
1→# AddItem Workflow
     2→
     3→Add an item to the queue system with automatic routing.
     4→
     5→## Trigger
     6→
     7→- "add to queue"
     8→- "queue task"
     9→- "/queue add"
    10→
    11→## Steps
    12→
    13→1. **Parse input** - Extract title, description, type from user request
    14→2. **Determine routing** - Items route to spec-pipeline by default (or approvals if `--spec` provided)
    15→3. **Add item** - Call QueueManager.add() with routing result
    16→4. **Context sufficiency check** - If description is detailed enough (>=200 chars, 2+ sentences), auto-advances to `researching`
    17→5. **Notify** - Confirm item added with ID and queue name
    18→
    19→## CLI Usage
    20→
    21→```bash
    22→# Basic add (routes to spec-pipeline, awaiting-context)
    23→bun run Tools/QueueManager.ts add "Task title" --desc "Details"
    24→
    25→# Detailed add (auto-advances to researching if sufficient)
    26→bun run Tools/QueueManager.ts add "Task title" --desc "Long detailed description with multiple sentences..."
    27→
    28→# With type (triggers routing rules)
    29→bun run Tools/QueueManager.ts add "Deploy v2" --type deploy
    30→
    31→# Skip spec-pipeline — attach existing spec (routes to approvals directly)
    32→bun run Tools/QueueManager.ts add "My Feature" --desc "Details" --spec plans/my-feature-spec.md
    33→
    34→# Direct to specific queue (approvals requires --spec)
    35→bun run Tools/QueueManager.ts add "Quick task" --queue spec-pipeline
    36→```
    37→
    38→## Routing Rules
    39→
    40→Items are routed based on patterns in `RoutingRules.yaml`:
    41→
    42→| Pattern | Queue | Notes |
    43→|---------|-------|-------|
    44→| `spec:*` | spec-pipeline | Direct pipeline entry |
    45→| `*` (default) | spec-pipeline | All items go through spec generation first |
    46→| `--spec <path>` | approvals | Items with existing specs bypass pipeline |
    47→
    48→## Context Sufficiency Heuristic
    49→
    50→When an item enters spec-pipeline, it checks if the description is detailed enough to skip the `awaiting-context` stage:
    51→
    52→**Auto-advances to `researching` when ALL of:**
    53→- Description >= 200 characters
    54→- Description contains 2+ sentences
    55→
    56→**Or when ANY of:**
    57→- Caller pre-supplied `notes` AND `researchGuidance` in context
    58→
    59→**Otherwise:** Item stays at `awaiting-context`. Use `/queue context` to provide structured guidance.
    60→
    61→## Programmatic Usage
    62→
    63→```typescript
    64→import { QueueManager } from './Tools/QueueManager.ts';
    65→
    66→const qm = new QueueManager();
    67→
    68→// Default: routes to spec-pipeline
    69→const id = await qm.add(
    70→  { title: "Review PR #42", description: "Auth refactor" },
    71→  { type: "approval", source: "GitHub" }
    72→);
    73→
    74→// With existing spec: routes to approvals directly
    75→const id2 = await qm.add(
    76→  { title: "My Feature", description: "Details" },
    77→  {
    78→    spec: {
    79→      id: "my-feature-spec",
    80→      path: "/absolute/path/to/my-feature-spec.md",
    81→      status: "approved",
    82→      approvedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    83→    }
    84→  }
    85→);
    86→```
    87→
    88→## Output
    89→
    90→Returns the item ID and confirms the target queue:
    91→
    92→```
    93→Added item: ml18vdke-n06t58
    94→Queue: spec-pipeline (awaiting-context)
    95→```
    96→
    97→Or if auto-advanced:
    98→```
    99→Added item: ml18vdke-n06t58
   100→Queue: spec-pipeline (researching)
   101→[spec-pipeline] Auto-advanced ml18vdke-n06t58 to researching (sufficient context)
   102→```
   103→

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * QueueManager.ts - Universal Queue Management for Kaya
     4→ *
     5→ * Core queue operations for the QueueRouter system. Provides add, list, update,
     6→ * remove, and query operations across all queues with JSONL file persistence.
     7→ *
     8→ * Uses CORE infrastructure tools:
     9→ * - StateManager for global queue state persistence
    10→ *
    11→ * Note: Individual queue files use JSONL format (one item per line) for
    12→ * efficient append operations. This is intentionally different from
    13→ * StateManager's single JSON file design.
    14→ *
    15→ * Usage:
    16→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts add --title "Task" --description "Details" [--queue name] [--priority 1-3]
    17→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts list [--queue name] [--status pending]
    18→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts get <id>
    19→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts update <id> --status completed
    20→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts remove <id>
    21→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts next [--queue name]
    22→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts complete <id> [--output "result"]
    23→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts fail <id> --error "reason"
    24→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts approve <id> [--notes "..."] [--reviewer "..."]
    25→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts reject <id> [--reason "..."] [--reviewer "..."]
    26→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts stats [--queue name]
    27→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts cleanup [--days 30]
    28→ *
    29→ * Progress Tracking:
    30→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts init-progress <id> <totalPhases>
    31→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts set-phase <id> <phase>
    32→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts complete-phase <id> <phase>
    33→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts update-isc <id> <criterion> <evidence>
    34→ *   bun run QueueManager.ts progress <id>
    35→ *
    36→ * @module QueueManager
    37→ */
    38→
    39→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, appendFileSync, readdirSync, renameSync } from "fs";
    40→import { join, basename } from "path";
    41→import { parse as parseYaml } from "yaml";
    42→import { z } from "zod";
    43→import { createStateManager, type StateManager } from "../../../lib/core/StateManager.ts";
    44→import { memoryStore } from "../../../lib/core/MemoryStore.ts";
    45→import { syncQueueStatus } from "../../LucidTasks/Tools/TaskManager.ts";
    46→
    47→// ============================================================================
    48→// Configuration
    49→// ============================================================================
    50→
    51→const KAYA_HOME = process.env.KAYA_HOME || join(process.env.HOME || "", ".claude");
    52→const QUEUES_DIR = join(KAYA_HOME, "MEMORY/QUEUES");
    53→const STATE_FILE = join(QUEUES_DIR, "state.json");
    54→const ROUTING_RULES_FILE = join(KAYA_HOME, "skills/QueueRouter/RoutingRules.yaml");
    55→const ARCHIVE_DIR = join(QUEUES_DIR, "archive");
    56→
    57→// ============================================================================
    58→// Types
    59→// ============================================================================
    60→
    61→export type QueueItemStatus =
    62→  | "pending"
    63→  | "in_progress"
    64→  | "awaiting_approval"
    65→  | "completed"
    66→  | "failed"
    67→  | "approved"
    68→  | "rejected"
    69→  // spec-pipeline statuses
    70→  | "awaiting-context"
    71→  | "researching"
    72→  | "generating-spec"
    73→  | "revision-needed"
    74→  | "escalated";
    75→
    76→/**
    77→ * Valid status transitions for the spec-pipeline queue.
    78→ * Enforced by validateSpecPipelineTransition().
    79→ */
    80→export const SPEC_PIPELINE_TRANSITIONS: Record<string, readonly string[]> = {
    81→  "awaiting-context": ["researching"],
    82→  "researching": ["generating-spec"],
    83→  "generating-spec": ["revision-needed"],
    84→  "revision-needed": ["researching", "escalated"],
    85→  "escalated": [], // terminal state — no auto-transitions
    86→} as const;
    87→
    88→/**
    89→ * Validate a status transition for spec-pipeline items.
    90→ * Returns null on success, or an error message string on failure.
    91→ */
    92→export function validateSpecPipelineTransition(
    93→  fromStatus: string,
    94→  toStatus: string
    95→): string | null {
    96→  const allowed = SPEC_PIPELINE_TRANSITIONS[fromStatus];
    97→  if (allowed === undefined) {
    98→    // fromStatus is not a spec-pipeline status — not our concern
    99→    return null;
   100→  }
